records.ety: [OE. hindere, AS. hinder, adv., behind; akin to OHG. hintar, prep., behind, G. hinter, Goth. hindar; orig. a comparative, and akin to AS. hine hence. See Hence, He, and cf. Hind, a., Hindmost.]

defs: Of or belonging to that part or end which is in the rear, or which follows; as, the hinder part of a wagon; the hinder parts of a horse.
quotes: He was in the hinder part of the ship.

records.ety: [OE. hindren, hinderen, AS. hindrian, fr. hinder behind; akin to D. hinderen, G. hindern, OHG. hintar<?/n, Icel. & Sw. hindra, Dan. hindre. See Hinder, a.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Hindered (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Hindering.]

defs: To keep back or behind; to prevent from starting or moving forward; to check; to retard; to obstruct; to bring to a full stop; -- often followed by from; as, an accident hindered the coach; drought hinders the growth of plants; to hinder me from going.
quotes: Them that were entering in ye hindered.

defs: To prevent or embarrass; to debar; to shut out.
quotes: What hinders younger brothers, being fathers of families, from having the same right?

defs: To interpose obstacles or impediments; to be a hindrance.
quotes: This objection hinders not but that the heroic action of some commander . . . may be written.
